skill: Linear inequalities in one or two variables
stem: An event planner is planning a banquet. It costs the event planner a onetime fee of $50 to rent the hall and $11.50 per attendee. The event planner has a budget of $400. What is the greatest number of attendees possible without exceeding the budget?
rationale: The correct answer is 30. Let x be the number of attendees. The total cost is 50 + 11.50x. Setting up the inequality 50 + 11.50x ≤ 400 and solving: 11.50x ≤ 350, so x ≤ 30.43... The greatest whole number of attendees is 30.
